title: 10 best herbal teas for blocked nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with blocked nose because it contains eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that effectively breaks down mucus and clears out nasal passages. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, making it easier to breathe through a congested nose. As the tea is absorbed into the bloodstream, its expectorant effects further aid in loosening and eliminating excess mucus, providing quick relief from stuffiness and congestion.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: This natural decongestant helps to break down mucus and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing.
Cineole: As an expectorant, cineole helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing blockages and sinus pressure.
Tannins: Tannins have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, further alleviating congestion and blocked noses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, roots help to reduce congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Bark: Containing expectorant properties, bark helps to loosen mucus and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tasmanian blue gum leaves or purchase dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in bulk stores.
Rinse the leaves with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris and pat dry with paper towels.
Combine one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 250ml boiling water in a teapot or infuser for five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional two to three minutes before serving hot, inhaling deeply through the nose to help ease congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which ease nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The menthol in peppermint oil opens up airways and loosens mucus, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the warm liquid soothes and relaxes the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and swelling. As a result, peppermint teas can provide effective relief from a blocked nose, making it an excellent natural remedy to combat common colds, allergies, and sinusitis.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion by opening up airways.
Menthol: Provides a cooling sensation that helps to loosen and clear out mucus and debris from the nasal passages, reducing stuffiness and promoting drainage.
Volatile oils: Inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause sinus infections and respiratory issues, helping to alleviate congestion and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other essential oils, peppermint roots are used to make teas that help ease congestion and sinus pressure.
Barks: The rough outer layer of the peppermint stem bark contains high levels of menthone, making it an effective ingredient for relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality peppermint leaves measuring about one tablespoon for every eight ounces of water.
Heat fresh or dried peppermint leaves in a saucepan filled with boiling water for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup containing honey and lemon to taste.
Allow the tea to steep for at least five minutes to release its natural oils and beneficial compounds.
Serve hot and enjoy as a soothing drink to help relieve nasal congestion and promote clear breathing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with blocked nose because its natural decongestant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, reducing nasal congestion. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe and calm the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thyme teas' warm and soothing properties can help to ease sinus pressure and tension, providing quick relief from blocked noses.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme tea, has natural decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in thyme tea, including cineole and borneol, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, allowing for easier breathing.

Antioxidants: Thyme tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the nasal mucosa from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that may contribute to blocked noses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used in herbal teas to help clear nasal congestion and sinus pressure due to their antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Thyme flowers add a soothing quality to teas, helping to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the measured thyme leaves and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired, and stir well to combine.
Drink the warm tea immediately after preparation to help relieve blocked nasal passages.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with blocked nose because it contains rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion. The tea's soothing and calming effects can also ease sinus pressure and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, lemon balm's gentle decongestant qualities can help to loosen and clear out mucus, leaving the nasal passages feeling clearer and more refreshed. As a result, drinking herbal lemon balm teas can provide much-needed relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea's antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, ease congestion, and combat the underlying viral infection causing the blockage.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of lemon balm tea help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relieving blocked nose symptoms.

Antihistaminic properties: Lemon balm tea's antihistaminic properties help to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can contribute to nasal congestion, itchiness, and runniness, providing relief from these uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to ease respiratory congestion and promote sinus drainage.
Flowers: With their sweet aroma and anti-inflammatory properties, flowers soothe nasal passages and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves or purchase dried ones for a total of one teaspoon per cup.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves into a teapot or infuser for every cup desired.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 195°f to 205°f and pour over the lemon balm leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy as is or with honey and lemon slices to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop teas helps with blocked nose because it contains expectorants that help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. The tea's natural anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for a clearer airflow. Additionally, hyssop's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause congestion. By soothing and calming the respiratory tract, herbal hyssop teas provide relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure, promoting healthy breathing and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of hyssop tea help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, allowing for easier drainage and relief from a blocked nose.

Antihistamine-like properties: Hyssop tea contains compounds that have antihistamine-like effects, which can help reduce congestion caused by allergies or colds by blocking histamine receptors and reducing nasal secretion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated nasal passages.
Buds: Contains bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves or 1 tablespoon dried herb for every 8 ounces water.
Bring filtered water to boiling point in a medium-sized pot and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add the harvested hyssop herb to the pot and let it steep for an additional 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Drink the tea immediately after preparation, ideally in small amounts every 2-3 hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The tea's compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and congestion, making it easier for air to flow freely through the nose. Additionally, licorice root has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out mucus, further relieving nasal blockages. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from stuffy noses without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

Flavonoids: Licorice tea contains flavonoids, which are antioxidants that can help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, including in the respiratory tract, reducing nasal blockage and congestion.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving blocked noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion.
Barks: Contain triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing nasal swelling and promoting drainage.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried licorice root powder or two teaspoons of fresh licorice root and add it to a heat-resistant cup.
Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot and pour it over the licorice root ingredients in the cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup discarding the solids and any excess sediment.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption and enjoy as is or sweetened with honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with blocked nose because it contains a unique compound called cineole, which has natural decongestant properties. When consumed as a tea, this compound is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the nasal passages, helping to thin out mucus and reduce inflammation. This can provide instant relief from congestion and sinus pressure, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell. The warm, comforting flavor of Ceylon cinnamon teas also helps to soothe the throat and sinuses, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ceylon cinnamon tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant property: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, including the nose, sinuses, and throat, reducing blockage and promoting easy breathing.
Antimicrobial property: Ceylon cinnamon tea has antimicrobial compounds that can help combat bacterial and viral infections that often cause nasal congestion, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in starch and essential oils, rhyzomes help to relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and opening up airways.
Barks: With its anti-inflammatory properties, barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant help to ease sinus pressure and clear mucus from the nose.
remedy_recipe: Boil one liter of filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan for 10 minutes to purify and heat the water.
Add two whole ceylon cinnamon sticks measuring about four inches long to the boiling water and simmer for 15 minutes to release essential oils.
Remove the saucepan from the heat source and let it steep for an additional five minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Strain the tea into a large ceramic mug using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the cinnamon sticks and any sediment.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, as it is best consumed warm but not hot to soothe nasal passages effectively for up to 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with blocked nose because its natural compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to have decongestant properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and alleviate congestion. Additionally, rosemary's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling in the nasal passages, further opening up airways and promoting clear breathing. This natural remedy provides relief from sinus pressure and congestion, making it an effective way to tackle a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Rosemary tea's ability to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages helps to alleviate a blocked nose, allowing for easier breathing.
Expectorant Properties: The expectorant properties of rosemary tea help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving sinus pressure and congestion.

Antihistamine-Like Effects: Rosemary's antihistamine-like compounds can help reduce histamine release, which is responsible for nasal congestion, itching, and sneezing, providing relief from these symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing congestion and opening up airways.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to thin mucus and reduce nasal congestion, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried rosemary leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities and debris.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water needed.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan until it reaches a rolling boil then reduce heat slightly.
Steep the rosemary leaves in the hot water for seven to ten minutes allowing flavors and oils to release.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with blocked nose because it contains a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. The tea's soothing properties also help to reduce inflammation in the sinuses, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, fennel's natural antispasmodic properties may help to relax the muscles in the nose, further reducing blockages and promoting a clear airway.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for a clearer airway and improved breathing.

Seeds: Containing a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 teaspoons of fresh fennel leaves or 1 teaspoon dried fennel flowers for each cup of boiling water.
Rinse the harvested fennel with cool running water and pat dry with a paper towel to remove any dirt or debris.
Bring one cup of pure filtered water to a boil in a medium-sized pot then add the rinsed and prepared fennel herb.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes allowing the fennel essence to infuse into the boiling water creating a soothing tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup discarding any solids and serve warm or chilled depending on personal preference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder teas helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. Barks: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease congestion in the sinuses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Rinse the harvested material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine one teaspoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers with one cup of boiling water steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large mug and discard solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it slowly while sitting comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. When brewed into a tea, these compounds are released into the airways, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant properties: Ginger has natural expectorant properties, which means it helps loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, including the nose and sinuses, making it easier to expel.

Antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds: Ginger contains antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that help combat infections and reduce oxidative stress in the nasal passages, promoting a healthy mucous membrane and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes help reduce congestion and ease sinus pressure.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant contain compounds that help thin mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots for at least an hour or overnight to remove excess moisture.
Grind 5g of dried ginger roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of ground ginger powder, letting it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for a few minutes before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper teas helps with blocked nose because of its natural expectorant properties. The capsaicin present in black pepper stimulates mucus production, making it easier to expel excess mucus and congestion from the nasal passages. As a result, herbal black pepper tea can help alleviate sinus pressure, reduce nasal congestion, and ease breathing. Additionally, the warm, soothing liquid can also help to relax the nasal tissues and promote drainage, providing quick relief from blocked nose discomfort.

Piperine: Has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with sinus pressure and congestion.
Caffeine: Acts as a natural decongestant and vasoconstrictor, helping to reduce nasal secretions and shrink swollen blood vessels in the nose, allowing for improved airflow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to relieve congestion and reduce nasal blockages.
Barks: The barks contain bioactive compounds that have natural decongestant properties, making them effective in alleviating a runny or stuffy nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried black pepper fruit and two cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five minutes in a teapot or infuser to release essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey as desired to enhance flavor and soothe congestion.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving warm and inhaling vapors directly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape teas helps with blocked nose because its soothing properties calm down the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Grape tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of dried vitis vinifera leaves and stems from your local apothecary or online supplier.
Rinse the dried vitis vinifera mixture thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine 2 teaspoons of the rinsed vitis vinifera mixture with 1 cup of boiling filtered water in a large tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the herbal blend to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids before serving.
Drink the warm tea 2-3 times daily as needed, ideally 30 minutes prior to meals to enhance its effectiveness.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried rose petals and boiling water for a soothing and relaxing tea.

Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove loose particles.

Drink two cups of rosa damascena tea three times a day to alleviate nasal congestion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried tilia cordata leaves according to seasonal availability and local regulations.

Steep the herb mixture in a tea infuser or directly in boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard solids after three to five minutes.

Allow the herbal tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.

Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these flowers help to relax the airways, ease respiratory issues, and alleviate sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Measure one tablespoon of dried origanum vulgare leaves per eight ounces boiling water.
Heat the measured water in a teapot or kettle until it reaches a rolling boil.
Add the measured origanum vulgare leaves to the boiling water and steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or mug to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming, taking two to three cups per day as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat blocked nose with herbal teas is by brewing a strong concoction of peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger. Drink the tea three to four times a day to help loosen congestion and reduce sinus pressure. Alternatively, try a chamomile or nettle tea to soothe and calm the nasal passages. These herbs work together to ease breathing and promote overall respiratory health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to blocked nose that are treated with herbal teas are sinusitis, allergies, and hay fever. Herbal teas such as peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger help to loosen mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages. Other herbs like thyme, rosemary, and sage have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated sinuses and promote healthy respiratory function. These teas can provide relief from sinus pressure, headaches, and postnasal drip associated with these conditions.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for blocked nose are a natural remedy that helps alleviate congestion and sinus pressure by thinning mucus and opening airways. They work by stimulating the body's natural defenses and promoting drainage. Examples include peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger teas, which help loosen mucus and ease breathing. By sipping on these teas, individuals with blocked noses can breathe easily and improve their overall quality of life.
